exercises.title: RUNNING TALLY
exercises.brief: A function that remembers its running total.
promptLines: Define **makeAccumulator()** returning a function. Each call **adds** its argument to a running total kept inside the closure and returns the **new** total. A fresh accumulator starts at **0**.
starterLines: // TODO: keep a total, return a function that adds to it

tests.name: accumulates across calls
instances are independent

exercises.hint: let total = 0 outside the returned function; inside do total += n; return total.
exercises.lore: The meter never resets. Every credit you burn, it remembers.

exercises.title: FLIP-FLOP RELAY
exercises.brief: A switch that flips on every pull.
promptLines: Define **toggle(a, b)** returning a function that **alternates** its return value each call: `a`, then `b`, then `a`, then `b`, and so on. The **first** call returns `a`.
starterLines: // TODO: remember which side is next, then flip

tests.name: alternates a then b
works with numbers
tests.code:
var f = toggle(1, 0);
assertEqual([f(), f(), f(), f(), f()], [1, 0, 1, 0, 1]);

tests.name: instances flip independently

exercises.hint: Keep a boolean flag in the closure; read the value, then invert the flag before returning.
exercises.lore: Pull the relay: light, dark, light, dark. The grid breathes in two states.

exercises.title: RATE GATE
exercises.brief: Allow a fixed number of passes, then deny.
promptLines: Define **limiter(max)** returning a function that returns `true` for its first `max` calls, then `false` for every call after that.
starterLines: // TODO: count the calls, allow only the first max

tests.name: allows max then denies
limit of 0 denies immediately
tests.code:
var gate = limiter(0);
assert(gate() === false, 'with max 0 the very first call is denied');

tests.name: separate gates count separately

exercises.hint: Track a used count; while used < max increment and return true, otherwise return false.
exercises.lore: The turnstile clicks twice, then locks. After that you climb the fence.

exercises.title: SINGLE FIRE
exercises.brief: Run it once; cache the result forever.
promptLines: Define **once(fn)** returning a wrapper. The **first** time the wrapper is called it runs `fn` (passing along the argument) and remembers the result. Every later call returns that **same** first result **without** calling `fn` again.
const boot = once(function (x) { runs++; return x * 2; });
starterLines: // TODO: return a wrapper that calls fn at most one time

tests.name: returns first result every time
fn runs at most once
caches non-number results

exercises.hint: Keep a called flag and a saved result; on the first call set the flag and store fn(x), then always return result.
exercises.lore: The ignition sequence runs one time. After that the engine just hums the same note.

exercises.title: ITERATED CHAIN
exercises.brief: Compose a function with itself n times.
promptLines: Define **applyN(fn, n)** returning a function that applies `fn` to its input **n** times in a row. With `n === 0` it returns the input unchanged (identity).
starterLines: // TODO: return a function that runs fn on its input n times

tests.name: applies fn n times
n = 0 is identity
tests.code:
var dbl = function (x) { return x * 2; };
assert(applyN(dbl, 0)(42) === 42, 'zero applications returns the input');

tests.name: works for any fn
tests.code:
var dbl = function (x) { return x * 2; };
assertEqual(applyN(dbl, 4)(1), 16);

tests.name: reusable on different inputs

exercises.hint: Return (x) => { let v = x; loop n times v = fn(v); return v; }. When n is 0 the loop never runs.
exercises.lore: Feed the signal back through the same gate, again and again. Each pass bends it further.
